notes: Data pass (approved 2026-07-04, unlocked by the .181–.183 device verify) — honest redesign routing for the 11 INDEX search entries that had no rd home. Under rd these previously dead-ended via the B4 guard (TRIAGE hits toasted + stayed on the Ref frame; most OPS hits degraded to the Deploy Command Center). KEYS: the 5 TRIAGE entries (down-link flowchart / dropping packets / –40dBm LOS / flapping / optic-not-recognized) → ref:'rf-optics' — content parity VERIFIED, not assumed: their cards physically live in #fs-triage (lines ~12450–12531), a fiber-sub panel that redesign_homeOptics re-homes into Ref›Optics — the ref: key lands the very same DOM nodes (one pill tap deep, consistent with every other ref: entry). OPS entries: 'CLI output analyzer' → ref:'rf-cli' (its snip contains 'port map', so post-B4 it mis-routed to the PORT MAP tool — the ref: key now wins and lands Ref›CLI where the parser lives); 'Port map validator' → tool:'portmap' (its snip lacked the 'port map' token the legacy opsTab sniffing needed); 'SOPs saved procedures' → ref:'rf-know' (KNOW is the canonical SOP door per the R-1 owner ruling); 'Optic scanner' + 'Optic inventory' → wk:'scan' (Work›Scan is the scan-flow home); 'BOM EDP analyzer' → tool:'bom' (explicit; previously worked via the guard's opsTab honor). MECHANISM: two rd-gated lines added in the search-hit onclick, mirroring the existing h.ref check — tool: → rd_openOpsTool(h.tool) (canonical Work ops-tool door), wk: → showWorkTab(h.wk); both read ONLY under redesign_isOn(), and legacy ignores unknown object keys → ?legacy=1 byte-identical by construction. VERIFICATION NOTE: no agent pass on this ship — the hook clones the long-live ref: pattern verbatim, and both door functions are the exact paths device-verified in the .181–.183 pass; keys derived from direct content inspection. LEGACY CURIOSITY (observed, untouched): the 5 TRIAGE entries carry page:'triage' but their content lives on pg-fiber›fs-triage — legacy search hits have always landed the TODAY page without that content; fixing that would change legacy behavior, so it waits for R1-era work. Then ?legacy=1: the same searches open exactly the pages they always did. ROLLBACK: revert the commit. Gates: 4 script blocks compile, CSS braces 3560 balanced, CRLF preserved, 3-stamp lockstep.
